intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios after an unexpected emergency rate hike.
intake.current_development: An emergency rate hike shocks funding markets, credit spreads, and rate expectations.
known_unknowns: Whether dealers can absorb the repositioning cleanly

summary: Authorities will prioritize market functioning if stress worsens

documents.rate-shock.md: The emergency rate hike triggers repricing across bonds and equities while liquidity stress rises.
documents.funding-stress.md: Funding markets widen immediately and investors debate whether policy credibility has improved or deteriorated.

case_title: Silicon Valley Bank failure and emergency backstop
historical_outcome: U.S. authorities protected depositors and launched emergency liquidity backstops to contain contagion and preserve confidence in the banking system.

sources.title: Joint Statement by the Department of the Treasury, Federal Reserve, and FDIC
The Federal Reserve's Response to the 2023 Banking Turmoil: The Bank Term Funding Program

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios after the March 2023 Silicon Valley Bank failure and emergency U.S. backstop actions.
intake.current_development: After Silicon Valley Bank failed, U.S. authorities protected depositors and launched the Bank Term Funding Program to backstop liquidity and stabilize confidence.
known_constraints: Financial stability and depositor confidence must be preserved
Authorities also need to preserve policy credibility
known_unknowns: Whether contagion will spread to other regional banks

summary: Authorities will prioritize market functioning and depositor confidence over short-term moral-hazard concerns

documents.joint-statement.md: Treasury, the Federal Reserve, and the FDIC took decisive actions to strengthen confidence, fully protect depositors, and preserve credit access after Silicon Valley Bank failed.
documents.btfp.md: The Federal Reserve established the Bank Term Funding Program to provide liquidity and help banks meet depositor needs during the 2023 banking turmoil.

case_title: Bank of England temporary gilt purchases during the 2022 gilt crisis
historical_outcome: The Bank of England intervened with temporary and targeted gilt purchases and liquidity support to restore orderly market conditions during the gilt market crisis.

sources.title: Asset Purchase Facility: Gilt Sales – Market Notice 29 September 2022
Bank of England statement on end of gilt market operations

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios during the September 2022 UK gilt market crisis and Bank of England intervention.
intake.current_development: After severe dysfunction in the long-dated gilt market and stress around liability-driven investment funds, the Bank of England launches temporary gilt purchases to restore orderly conditions.
known_constraints: Financial stability and orderly market functioning must be restored quickly
Authorities also need to preserve monetary policy credibility
known_unknowns: Whether the intervention will contain contagion without becoming a permanent backstop

summary: Authorities will prioritize financial stability and market functioning over a rigid commitment to planned balance-sheet sales during the acute stress window

documents.boe-market-notice.md: The Bank of England said it would conduct temporary purchases of long-dated UK government bonds to restore orderly market conditions and postponed planned gilt sales.
documents.boe-end-operations.md: The Bank later said the temporary and targeted gilt purchases had increased market resilience and were intended to give liability-driven investment funds time to address risks.

case_title: Credit Suisse crisis and the 2023 UBS rescue package
historical_outcome: Swiss authorities used extraordinary support and a forced merger to stabilize Credit Suisse and contain broader market disruption.

sources.title: FINMA approves merger of UBS and Credit Suisse
Übernahme der Credit Suisse durch die UBS

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios during the March 2023 Credit Suisse crisis and rescue merger with UBS.
intake.current_development: A crisis of confidence at Credit Suisse triggers extraordinary liquidity support, official guarantees, and a forced takeover by UBS to preserve stability.
known_constraints: Authorities must preserve depositor confidence and market functioning quickly
The rescue package has to limit wider contagion across the financial system
known_unknowns: Whether the rescue package will stabilize confidence without creating broader market spillovers

summary: Authorities will prioritize a coordinated stabilization package over a passive wait-and-see approach

documents.finma-merger.md: FINMA said the takeover of Credit Suisse by UBS, together with official support measures, would ensure stability for customers and the Swiss financial centre.
documents.federal-support.md: Swiss authorities described an emergency package that enabled the UBS takeover of Credit Suisse and prevented greater harm to financial markets.

case_title: First Republic failure and FDIC-assisted transfer
historical_outcome: Authorities resolved First Republic through FDIC receivership and a rapid transfer to JPMorgan in order to protect depositors and contain further banking contagion.

sources.title: JPMorgan Chase Bank, National Association, Columbus, Ohio Assumes All the Deposits of First Republic Bank, San Francisco, California
FDIC Releases Report Detailing Supervision of the Former First Republic Bank, San Francisco, California

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios after the May 2023 failure of First Republic Bank and the FDIC-assisted transfer to JPMorgan.
intake.current_development: First Republic fails after a confidence crisis, the FDIC steps in as receiver, and substantially all deposits and assets move to JPMorgan.
known_constraints: Depositor confidence and banking-system stability must be preserved
Authorities need to limit spillovers from the spring 2023 bank failures
known_unknowns: Whether the transfer will fully contain contagion pressure at other regional banks

summary: Authorities will prioritize depositor protection and confidence stabilization over moral-hazard messaging during the acute phase

documents.fdic-transfer.md: The FDIC said First Republic was closed and all deposits plus substantially all assets were transferred to JPMorgan to protect depositors and minimize disruption.
documents.fdic-review.md: The FDIC later said a loss of market and depositor confidence following the March 2023 bank failures was the primary cause of First Republic's failure.

case_title: Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility during March 2020 market stress
historical_outcome: The Federal Reserve answered acute short-term funding stress by launching targeted liquidity facilities to support money market functioning rather than relying on pure guidance alone.

sources.title: Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility

intake.event_framing: Assess market scenarios during the March 2020 dash-for-cash period when the Federal Reserve launched emergency short-term funding facilities.
intake.current_development: Acute funding stress and redemption pressure in short-term markets push the Federal Reserve to launch emergency facilities to restore liquidity and market functioning.
known_constraints: Short-term funding markets need to keep functioning for households and businesses
Authorities need to relieve liquidity stress quickly
known_unknowns: Whether targeted liquidity facilities will be enough to restore confidence in money markets

summary: Authorities will use emergency liquidity tools rapidly rather than waiting for markets to self-correct

documents.mmlf.md: The Federal Reserve established the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility to support the flow of credit and help money market funds meet redemption pressure.
documents.cpff.md: The Federal Reserve established the Commercial Paper Funding Facility to support the issuance market for short-term debt and keep credit flowing to households and businesses.
